violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. PIC did successfully complete a Food Safety Course and test. Application for Certified Food Safety Manager License to be submitted as soon as possible. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

The high temp. rinse cycle pressure gauge was observed at 10psi during the rinse cycle. The pressure gauge is to read 15-25psi as required for proper high temperature sanitizing (dish machine observed to be properly sanitizing as indicated by thermo-label during inspection). Pressure guage is to be adjusted/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The ceiling in the kitchen area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are SMOOTH and EASILY CLEANABLE. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

The following equipment associated with the high temp. dish machine were observed in need of repair/replacement: - The high temp. rinse cycle pressure gauge was observed at 10psi during the rinse cycle. The pressure gauge is to read 15-25psi as required for proper high temperature sanitizing (dish machine observed to be properly sanitizing as indicated by thermo-label during inspection). - During the high temp rinse cycle, water was observed to be leaking from the vacuum breaker located next to the high temp pressure gauge. Equipment is to be adjusted/replaced and maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2025

The ceiling in the kitchen area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are SMOOTH and EASILY CLEANABLE. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025

The ceiling in the kitchen area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are SMOOTH and EASILY CLEANABLE. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

Sliced Cheese and Shredded Cheese were observed not marked with the date of preparation/thawing. Sliced cheeses and Shredded Cheeses are to be dated with the date of opening and held for no longer than 7 days from the date of opening unless frozen. If food item is frozen, the opening of the packaging is to be counted as day 1, and when the food item is pulled from the freezer for thawing, the item is to be redated/held for use for no longer than 6 days. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2024
